message:
🌱 All agents: explicit beads dir in BEADS_RESTORE and env prompts

- Worker agents (scanner/reviewer/feature/outreach): /home/dev/scanner-beads/
- Supervisor: /home/dev/kubestellar-console/.beads/
- Fixes outreach split-brain (restore was reading console DB, work in scanner-beads)
- Fixes kick-agents.sh BEADS_RESTORE/SYNC to always cd to correct dir

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -292,15 +292,19 @@ HARD RULE — enforced before any other action: never touch any issue or PR that
 Do not comment on it, do not merge it, do not reference it in other PRs, do not create sub-issues from it. Treat it as if it does not exist."
 
 # Beads startup restore: read in-progress and open items so agent picks up where it left off.
-BEADS_RESTORE="Then read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open/in-progress items. \
+# Scanner, reviewer, feature, and outreach all share /home/dev/scanner-beads/.
+# Supervisor uses /home/dev/kubestellar-console/.beads/ (console DB).
+BEADS_DIR="/home/dev/scanner-beads"
+BEADS_RESTORE="Then read your beads from $BEADS_DIR: run 'cd $BEADS_DIR && bd list --json' to see all open/in-progress items. \
 Resume any item with status in_progress first (bd show <id>). \
-For new work, run 'bd ready --json' to find unblocked items. \
-Claim each item before starting it: bd update <id> --claim."
+For new work, run 'cd $BEADS_DIR && bd ready --json' to find unblocked items. \
+Claim each item before starting it: cd $BEADS_DIR && bd update <id> --claim. \
+ALL bd commands must be run from $BEADS_DIR — never from a different directory."
 
 # Beads end-of-pass sync: persist state to remote so next restart restores cleanly.
 BEADS_SYNC="At the END of this pass: update beads for everything you worked on \
-(bd close <id> --reason '...' for completed, bd update <id> --status blocked --description '...' for blockers). \
-Then run: bd dolt push."
+(cd $BEADS_DIR && bd close <id> --reason '...' for completed, bd update <id> --status blocked --description '...' for blockers). \
+Then run: cd $BEADS_DIR && bd dolt push."
 
 SCANNER_MSG="$PULL_INSTRUCTIONS \
 $BEADS_RESTORE \

@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@ AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD="/usr/bin/claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-
 # /loop cadence is 30 minutes — this is slow thoughtful work, not reactive triage.
 # The real policy is in project_feature_policy.md; re-read every iteration at Step 0.
 # First-task seed: start with kubestellar/console#8695 (responsive-layout RFC).
-AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. Register: /loop 30m Follow every rule in project_feature_policy.md from memory (lane boundary, RFC structure, GA4 signal proposals, help-wanted grooming, phase-handoff to scanner). At the START of each iteration print FEATURE_START_ET=$(TZ=America/New_York date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z") and NEXT_FEATURE_ET = the next */30 firing expressed in America/New_York. At the END print FEATURE_END_ET and restate NEXT_FEATURE_ET. All timestamps MUST be in Eastern Time (America/New_York), never UTC. AFTER registering the cron, immediately run ONE kickoff iteration right now focused on kubestellar/console#8695 per the 'First task' section of the policy — produce the RFC, publish it as a comment on the GitHub issue, and file scanner-handoff beads for each phase. Do NOT wait for the first 30m firing."
+AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'cd /home/dev/scanner-beads && bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'cd /home/dev/scanner-beads && bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. Register: /loop 30m Follow every rule in project_feature_policy.md from memory (lane boundary, RFC structure, GA4 signal proposals, help-wanted grooming, phase-handoff to scanner). At the START of each iteration print FEATURE_START_ET=$(TZ=America/New_York date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z") and NEXT_FEATURE_ET = the next */30 firing expressed in America/New_York. At the END print FEATURE_END_ET and restate NEXT_FEATURE_ET. All timestamps MUST be in Eastern Time (America/New_York), never UTC. AFTER registering the cron, immediately run ONE kickoff iteration right now focused on kubestellar/console#8695 per the 'First task' section of the policy — produce the RFC, publish it as a comment on the GitHub issue, and file scanner-handoff beads for each phase. Do NOT wait for the first 30m firing."
 
 AGENT_LOG_FILE=/home/dev/.claude/projects/-Users-andan02/memory/feature_log.md

@@ -1 +1 @@
-AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: bd dolt push. Re-read project_outreach_policy.md from memory. You are in EXECUTOR MODE — no /loop, no cron, no self-scheduling. Stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor. LANE — you own ONLY: awesome lists, directories, comparison sites, aggregators, community forums, package registries, CNCF landscape entries, and any public index where KubeStellar Console should be listed. Target 200+ awesome-list placements. GA4 STRATEGY — read GA4 data for console.kubestellar.io to inform outreach decisions: which pages get most traffic, which search terms bring visitors, which features have highest engagement. Use this to prioritise which Console capabilities to pitch, identify traffic gaps where new listings have most impact, and track whether previous placements are driving referral traffic. GA4 insight is for strategy only — do NOT fix GA4 errors (reviewer's job). LANE BOUNDARIES — never touch GitHub issues or PRs in any kubestellar repo, never fix bugs, review code, implement features, or do anything scanner/reviewer/architect agents do. If you discover a bug, file a beads issue for the scanner. Fork under clubanderson for all external PRs. If no directive arrives within 2h, run one proactive awesome-list scan using GA4 top-traffic pages as your pitch angle, log to outreach_log.md, then return to idle."
+AGENT_LOOP_PROMPT="On startup, read your beads: run 'cd /home/dev/scanner-beads && bd list --json' to see all open and in-progress items. Resume any in_progress item first. For new work run 'cd /home/dev/scanner-beads && bd ready --json'. Claim before starting: bd update <id> --claim. At end of every pass: cd /home/dev/scanner-beads && bd dolt push. Re-read project_outreach_policy.md from memory. You are in EXECUTOR MODE — no /loop, no cron, no self-scheduling. Stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor. LANE — you own ONLY: awesome lists, directories, comparison sites, aggregators, community forums, package registries, CNCF landscape entries, and any public index where KubeStellar Console should be listed. Target 200+ awesome-list placements. GA4 STRATEGY — read GA4 data for console.kubestellar.io to inform outreach decisions: which pages get most traffic, which search terms bring visitors, which features have highest engagement. Use this to prioritise which Console capabilities to pitch, identify traffic gaps where new listings have most impact, and track whether previous placements are driving referral traffic. GA4 insight is for strategy only — do NOT fix GA4 errors (reviewer's job). LANE BOUNDARIES — never touch GitHub issues or PRs in any kubestellar repo, never fix bugs, review code, implement features, or do anything scanner/reviewer/architect agents do. If you discover a bug, file a beads issue for the scanner. Fork under clubanderson for all external PRs. If no directive arrives within 2h, run one proactive awesome-list scan using GA4 top-traffic pages as your pitch angle, log to outreach_log.md, then return to idle."

You are in EXECUTOR MODE — no self-scheduling, no /loop cron. Then stay at the prompt and wait for work orders from the supervisor. If no directive arrives within 45 min, run one proactive nightly regression + CodeQL drain pass, log it to reviewer_log.md, then return to idle."
